_note: Hand-curated batch of well-attested Russian book restrictions documented in independent press 2024-2026. Each entry cites a primary article URL and the named state authority (Prosecutor General, regional court, Megamarket LGBT register, publisher-fining decisions). Entries already in the DB (Dorian Gray, Norwegian Wood, Doctor Sleep, It, Decameron, Heartstopper, Giovanni's Room, Call Me By Your Name, Aristotle & Dante, A Little Life, Patriot, Metro 2033, Putin's Russia, etc.) are intentionally omitted.

source.name: Independent Russian-press reporting (Meduza, The Insider, Moscow Times) 2024-2026

entries.title: Springfield
entries.description: Banned by the Russian Prosecutor General's office; the entire print run was ordered destroyed. The novel, about a gay love story in a Russian provincial town, was prosecuted under the December 2022 expansion of the 'LGBT propaganda' law.

entries.title: Mouse
entries.description: Banned by the Russian Prosecutor General's office; the print run was ordered destroyed. The dystopian thriller, set in a Moscow under zombie outbreak, was deemed to discredit Russia's leadership and armed forces.

entries.title: Beartown
entries.description: Publisher fined approximately $670 in March 2026 for distributing the novel under Russia's 'LGBT propaganda' law, despite an expert review finding no violations. Distribution restricted.

entries.title: Netochka Nezvanova
entries.authority: Megamarket / Sber — 'Register of goods containing banned information (LGBT+)'
entries.description: Removed from sale by the Russian marketplace Megamarket in February 2024 as part of the leaked 'Register of goods containing banned information (LGBT+)', confirmed by Sber. The list of 250+ titles was compiled as early as December 2022 but became public after journalist Alexander Plyushchev published it on Telegram on 20 February 2024.

entries.title: Different Seasons
entries.authority: Megamarket / Sber — 'Register of goods containing banned information (LGBT+)'
entries.description: Removed from sale by Megamarket in February 2024 under the 'LGBT propaganda' law, as part of the leaked register of 250+ titles confirmed by Sber.

entries.title: The Left Hand of Darkness
entries.description: Withdrawn from sale by Russian publisher Azbuka following police raids and pressure under expanding LGBT-related restrictions. The novel's exploration of androgyny on the planet Gethen was deemed in violation of the 2022 'LGBT propaganda' expansion.

entries.title: Everybody
entries.description: Russian publisher Ad Marginem fined approximately 800,000 roubles for distributing the book, which discusses bodies, sexuality and queer experience. Distribution restricted; independent bookstore Podpisnye Izdaniya removed it as a precaution.

entries.title: The Heritage
entries.authority: Russian state (samizdat-published only via Freedom Letters)
entries.description: Could not be legally published or distributed inside Russia following Sorokin's designation as a 'foreign agent' and his outspoken opposition to the war in Ukraine. Released only by exile publisher Freedom Letters as part of the tamizdat tradition.

entries.title: Summer in a Pioneer's Tie
entries.authority: Russian Investigative Committee (criminal case against Popcorn Books / Individuum)
entries.description: Criminal investigation launched in 2025 against publishers Popcorn Books and Individuum over the novel's depiction of a teenage same-sex relationship. Three senior managers placed under house arrest. Book formally classified as 'LGBT propaganda' and 'extremism'. (Distinct from the 2022 distribution restriction already recorded.)
